Skip to contents
evfuse 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Spatial Data Fusion for Extreme Sea Levels

Fit Stage 2 spatial model via MLE

Source: R/spatial_model.R
fit_spatial_model.Rd

Estimates beta (6x1), A (6x6 lower triangular), and rho (6x1) by maximizing the Gaussian likelihood from Eq. (7) of Russell et al. (2020), extended to handle partial observations. NOAA sites observe components 1-3 and ADCIRC sites observe components 4-6 of the 6-dimensional parameter vector.

Usage

fit_spatial_model(
  stage1,
  dat,
  W_tap,
  D,
  start = NULL,
  method = "L-BFGS-B",
  control = list(maxit = 1000, trace = 1),
  check_gradient = FALSE
)

Arguments

stage1

An evfuse_stage1 object from fit_gev_all or fit_gev_detrended.

dat

An evfuse_data object.

W_tap

Tapered covariance matrix from taper_W.

D

Distance matrix from compute_distances.

start

Optional named list of starting values for beta, A, rho.

method

Optimization method for optim (default "L-BFGS-B").

control

Control list passed to optim.

check_gradient

If TRUE, verify analytic gradient against numerical finite differences at the starting values before optimizing.

Value

A list with components:

beta

Estimated mean vector (6x1).

A

Estimated lower triangular matrix (6x6).

rho

Estimated range parameters (6x1).

Sigma

Estimated covariance matrix at observed sites.

optim_result

Raw output from optim.

grad_check

If check_gradient = TRUE, the output of verify_gradient.

Details

Uses an analytic gradient derived from the coregionalization structure (see Sections 2, 5, 6 of the gradient notes). The key identity is that dSigma/dA_ab and dSigma/drho_k are rank-1 Kronecker products, enabling O(n_obs^2) gradient computation per parameter instead of O(n_obs^3).

On this page

Developed by Brian N. White, Brian Blanton, Rick Luettich, Richard L. Smith.
